headline: Transcript: Japan’s transformational coalition deal
summary: Marc Filippino talks to Leo Lewis and Antoine Gara
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘Japan’s transformational coalition deal’

Marc FilippinoGood morning from the Financial Times. Today is Tuesday, October 21st, and this is your FT News Briefing. European governments are rallying behind Volodymyr Zelenskyy. And Japan is entering a new era. Plus, the CEO of Apollo thinks that Europe is having a hard time getting out of its own way.

Marc Rowan voice clipI see Europe a little bit at war with itself with respect to financial regulation.

Marc FilippinoI’m Marc Filippino, and here’s the news you need to start your day.

[MUSIC PLAYING]Leaders in Europe are scrambling to secure a deal on the use of Russia’s frozen assets. They’re hoping to use them for a €140bn loan to Ukraine. This comes after US President Donald Trump and his Ukrainian counterpart, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, held a tense meeting on Friday. Trump pressured Zelenskyy to accept Russia’s deal to end its war.
